data.title: CGI awarded task orders for visa application processing services
data.description:
CGI Inc. (NYSE:GIB) subsidiary has been awarded three task orders by the U.S. Department of State to support end-to-end visa application processing services for the Bureau of Consular Affairs in 25 countries across Europe and Asia.

These task orders were awarded under CGI's Global Support Strategy for Overseas Consular Support Services 2.0 (GSS 2.0) indefinite-delivery, indefinite-quantity multi-award prime contract. They have a combined 2-year base period funded value of US$37.4M, and a combined total contract value of US$137.3M.

Under these task orders, CGI will continue to provide critical processing support for non-immigrant and immigrant visas through 2032 at U.S. embassies and consulates in 25 countries across Europe and Asia.

The company will leverage its CGI Atlas360 solution to provide visa applicants the opportunity to manage their application process, including inquiries, scheduling and document tracking.

data.title: Cadence Design Systems, Synopsys in focus as Mizuho starts coverage
data.description:
• Cadence Design Systems (NASDAQ:CDNS) and Synopsys (NASDAQ:SNPS) were in focus on Tuesday as research firm Mizuho Securities started coverage on the electronic design automation companies, putting an Outperform rating on each.
  • Cadence Design was fractionally positive in premarket trading, while Synopsys was fractionally negative.
  • The electronic design automation market is seen as a "stable oligopoly," dominated by Cadence Design, Synopsys and Siemens-Mentor, with all three having "durable" revenue streams and high margins, due to how important EDA has become beyond the semiconductor market, Mizuho analyst Siti Panigrahi said. Panigrahi has a $325 price target on Cadence Design and a $650 price target on Synopsys.
  • Given how design complexity has increased in a number of areas — such as hyperscalers, mobile and more — many companies are relying on EDA to help build their infrastructure and devices to support generative artificial intelligence and, thus, boost growth, Panigrahi added.
  • And while the macro environment is still uncertain, the AI revolution is set to be a long-term secular tailwind (perhaps to the tune of $100T) and both Cadence Design and Synopsys are seen as attractive and underappreciated investment opportunities, Panigrahi explained.

data.title: Moody's declares $0.85 dividend
data.description:
• Moody's (NYSE:MCO) declared $0.85/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 0.7%
  • Payable Dec. 13; for shareholders of record Nov. 22; ex-div Nov. 22.
  • See MCO Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Dime Community Bancshares GAAP EPS of $0.29 misses by $0.12, revenue of $87.55M in-line
data.description:
• Dime Community Bancshares press release (NASDAQ:DCOM): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.29 misses by $0.12.
  • Revenue of $87.55M (+3.7% Y/Y) in-line.
  • Total deposits increased $389 million compared to the second quarter of 2024.
  • Core deposits (excluding brokered and time deposits) increased $505 million compared to the second quarter of 2024.
  • The ratio of average non-interest-bearing deposits to average total deposits for the third quarter was 29% compared to 28% for the second quarter of 2024.
  • The allowance for credit losses to total loans increased to 0.78% at the end of the third quarter compared to 0.72% for the prior quarter.
  • The Company’s total risk based capital ratio increased to 14.76% at the end of the third quarter, compared to 14.46% for the prior quarter.

data.title: iRhythm gains after FDA nod for Zio AT updates
data.description:
• iRhythm Technologies (NASDAQ:IRTC) traded ~17% higher in the premarket on Tuesday after the digital healthcare company announced that the U.S. FDA cleared certain modifications to its cardiac monitoring device, Zio AT.
  • The FDA has cleared the company’s 510((k)) submission related to the design changes made via letter to file, iRhythm (IRTM) said.
  • “This clearance is related to modifications and certain enhancements to our Zio AT device previously made via letter to file,” CEO Quentin Blackford added.
  • The approval was related to the first of two 510((k)) submissions the company submitted for FDA review early this year. “We look forward to hearing about our second 510((k)) in the near future,” Blackford added.
  • In 2023, the FDA warned iRhythm (NASDAQ:IRTC) over certain changes and modifications made to the Zio AT System without 510((k)) submissions.

data.title: Teledyne Technologies' unit gets $91M contract from U.S. Army
data.description:
• Teledyne Technologies' (NYSE:TDY) unit has won a five-year contract worth up to $91 million to provide its nano drones to the U.S. army.
  • Teledyne FLIR has received $25 million in initial orders, which will cover the delivery of the first tranche of Black Hornet 4 drones, as well as controllers, spare parts, and training.
